subject: Public Health
title: Community-Led Total Sanitation
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Community-Led Total Sanitation.

hook.text: Instead of building toilets for people, one approach triggers communities to solve sanitation themselves.

buildup.text: Community-Led Total Sanitation (CLTS) uses shame and pride—not subsidies—to motivate collective action.

discovery.text: Facilitators walk communities through their open defecation areas—making the invisible health risk visible.

twist.text: CLTS has achieved open defecation-free status in thousands of communities across 70 countries.

climax.text: Critics argue the shame-based approach can violate dignity—ethical adaptations are evolving.

punchline.text: When communities own the problem, they build the solution.

quiz.question: How does Community-Led Total Sanitation work?
options: It triggers communities to solve sanitation collectively through awareness
It provides free toilets to every household
It uses fines to enforce toilet construction
